title: Roasted Crisp British Potato Gratin
description: A classic British dish featuring charred, crisp potatoes in a rich parmesan cheese sauce, perfect for a lazy Sunday. This ovo-vegetarian friendly recipe is sure to become a favorite.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Peel the potatoes and slice them into 1/8 inch thick rounds.
In a large bowl, toss the potato slices with the rendered fat, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
In a separate bowl, mix the grated parmesan cheese, minced garlic, and chopped fresh thyme.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of overlapping potato slices.
Sprinkle a third of the parmesan cheese mixture over the potatoes, followed by a third of the sliced onions.
Repeat the layering process two more times, finishing with a layer of potatoes on top.
In a small bowl, whisk together the eggs and double cream, then pour the mixture over the potatoes.
Bake the gratin in the preheated oven for 45 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the potatoes are tender.
Remove the gratin from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
